steps.title: Identify the series with internal punctuation
steps.content: The blank separates two appositive noun phrases. The left side is an appositive description: “Italian Renaissance painter of the Venetian School.” The right side is another: “Flemish mannerist painter of the Antwerp School.” These descriptions belong to different people in a coordinate series, and the blank marks where one person's description ends and the next person's information begins. Both descriptions follow a name and comma, so each item has the structure [NAME], [DESCRIPTION].

steps.title: Apply the complex-series punctuation rule
steps.content: When series items contain internal commas, use semicolons (not commas) to separate them. The blank requires a semicolon after “School,” then “Adriaen Brouwer,” with a comma before his description. Choice B—”School; Adriaen Brouwer,”—provides this structure. The others violate the rule by either using a comma instead of a semicolon, placing the semicolon between a name and description, or omitting punctuation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
